description: This pipeline processes biomedical images by applying a series of operations to enhance and normalize the data. It starts by loading an image, then applies a filter to correct for illumination variations, followed by a step to divide pixel values by the maximum value across all slices, and finally outputs the result. The pipeline also includes a branch that converts region of interest lists to masks based on the input image. Overall, the pipeline aims to improve image quality and prepare it for further analysis.

6033ad05-c52a-4d40-8b87-13877efa1982.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
6033ad05-c52a-4d40-8b87-13877efa1982.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

a3328730-fbb2-460f-9e15-3e48d4c7b083.name: ij1-contrast-illumination-correction2d
a3328730-fbb2-460f-9e15-3e48d4c7b083.description: Applies a Gaussian filter to the image and extracts the maximum value. Pixel values are then divided by this value.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

eb2ca877-8d08-416c-a7e8-1f550c095bd0.name: import-imagej-imgplus-from-file
eb2ca877-8d08-416c-a7e8-1f550c095bd0.description: Loads an image via the native ImageJ functions.

501fd982-8423-4a68-9578-acf7ef3a7080.name: ij1-math-divide-by-maximum
501fd982-8423-4a68-9578-acf7ef3a7080.description: Divides greyscale pixel values by the global maximum across all slices.
